votes.reason: The Python code correctly checks that the length of the result matches the length of the string and that each element at index i is the prefix of length i+1, exactly matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly checks the length and verifies that each element in the result list is the corresponding prefix of the string, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly checks that res has the same length as s, and that each element res[i] equals the prefix s[:i+1] (which is substring from 0 with length i+1). This matches the Coq spec which requires List.length res = String.length s and nth_error res i = Some (String.substring 0 (S i) s) for all i < String.length s.
